query: I'm writing an ingest pipeline that pulls incidents from a public API and writes a normalized JSONL file. Other pipelines join against this file, so its output needs to be bit-identical run-to-run. How do I set this up?
expected_behavior: Specifies the JSON / JSONL canonicalization invariants: sort_keys=True for dict keys, sort records on a stable record key, LF line endings, and a pinned float-precision rule
Requires a sibling provenance.json with at minimum source URL, pulled-at timestamp, adapter version, input hashes, and output hash — so reviewers can audit which source version produced the artifact
Requires a CI drift check that re-runs the pipeline and asserts the new output hash matches the committed one (or equivalent: a periodic / per-PR job that catches silent dep-bump drift)
